绷.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 朋: pulling thread taut, so 绷 means to stretch tight or strap.

sentence.en: The doctor wraps a bandage on him.

绸.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 周: woven silk, so 绸 means silk cloth or satin.

sentence.en: This dress is made of silk.

绺.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 咎: a small bundle of threads, so 绺 means a skein or lock.

sentence.en: She cut off a lock of hair.

绾.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 官: tying threads up neatly, so 绾 means to coil or bind up.

sentence.en: She tied her hair up.

缄.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 咸: binding a letter shut, so 缄 means to seal.

sentence.en: He stayed silent the whole time.

缓.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 爰: a loosely held thread, so 缓 means slow or to slow down.

sentence.en: The train started moving slowly.

缕.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 娄: a single thin thread, so 缕 means a strand or wisp.

sentence.en: A wisp of smoke drifted into the sky.

编.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 扁: weaving threads together, so 编 means to weave, knit, or compile.

sentence.en: She can weave a small basket.

缘.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 彖: the thread that links people, so 缘 means fate, bond, or reason.

sentence.en: For some reason, he did not come.

缚.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 尃: using cord to bind, so 缚 means to tie up.

sentence.en: The rope tied up the box.

缠.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 㢆: thread wound around and around, so 缠 means to wrap or entangle.

sentence.en: The vine twines around the tree.

缩.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 宿: thread pulling in, so 缩 means to shrink or pull back.

sentence.en: The sweater shrank after washing.

缫.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 巢: drawing silk from cocoons, so 缫 means to reel silk.

sentence.en: The workers are reeling silk.

缴.origin: 纟 (thread) plus 敫: handing in what is owed, so 缴 means to pay or submit.

sentence.en: I am going to pay the tuition.

缺.origin: 缶 (jar) plus 夬: a broken jar with a piece missing, so 缺 means to lack.

word.en: to lack, to be short of

sentence.en: We are short of time.

罐.origin: 缶 (jar) plus 雚: 罐 means a jar, jug, or pot.

sentence.en: There is a jar on the table.

网.origin: Looks like a net stretched on a frame: 网 means net or web.

sentence.en: The fisherman casts the net.

罗.origin: 罒 (net) over 夕: a fine net for catching birds, so 罗 means net or to gather.

word.en: to list out, to set out

sentence.en: Fruit is laid out on the table.

罚.origin: 罒 (net) over words and 刂 (knife): catching wrongdoers, so 罚 means to punish or fine.

sentence.en: Doing wrong gets you punished.

罢.origin: 罒 (net) over 去: caught in a net, then let go, so 罢 means to stop or cease.

sentence.en: We are just resting, that's all.

罩.origin: 罒 (net) over 卓: a net-like cover, so 罩 means a cover or to cover over.

sentence.en: Put on a mask when going out.

罪.origin: 罒 (net) over 非 (wrong): caught in a net of wrongdoing, so 罪 means crime or sin.

sentence.en: Stealing is a crime.

置.origin: 罒 (net) over 直: setting things in their place, so 置 means to place or set up.

sentence.en: Please sit back in your seat.

署.origin: 罒 (net) over 者: an office that organizes people, so 署 means a bureau or to sign.

sentence.en: They arranged the tasks.

羞.origin: 羊 (sheep) plus 丑: 羞 means shy, ashamed, or shame.

sentence.en: The little sister is very shy.

羽.origin: Two feathers side by side: 羽 means feather or plume.

sentence.en: There is a feather on the ground.

翁.origin: 公 over 羽 (feather): 翁 means an old man.

sentence.en: An old man sits by the river.

翅.origin: 支 plus 羽 (feather): the feathered parts a bird flies with, so 翅 means wings.

sentence.en: The little bird flaps its wings.

翘.origin: 尧 plus 羽 (feather): feathers lifting up, so 翘 means to tilt up or stick up.

word.en: to tilt up, to stick up

sentence.en: The kitten sticks its tail up.

翻.origin: 番 plus 羽 (wings): flipping like a bird turning over, so 翻 means to turn over or flip.

word.en: to turn the pages of a book

sentence.en: Please turn to page ten.

翼.origin: 羽 (feather) over 異: the feathered wings of a bird or plane, so 翼 means wing.

sentence.en: The airplane's wings are very long.

耀.origin: 光 (light) plus 翟: a bright shining tail, so 耀 means to shine or dazzle.

sentence.en: The sunlight is dazzling.

耍.origin: 而 over 女: 耍 means to play, frolic, or fool around.

sentence.en: The children are playing in the yard.

耐.origin: 而 plus 寸: 耐 means to endure or to be able to bear.

sentence.en: The teacher is very patient.

耕.origin: 耒 (plow) plus 井: working the fields with a plow, so 耕 means to plow or farm.

sentence.en: The farmer plows the field.

耗.origin: 耒 (plow) plus 毛: 耗 means to use up or consume.

sentence.en: Running uses up energy.

耙.origin: 耒 (plow) plus 巴: a farm tool with teeth, so 耙 means a rake or to rake.

sentence.en: He rakes the grass with a rake.

耧.origin: 耒 (plow) plus 娄: a tool pulled along to sow seeds, a seed drill.

sentence.en: Sowing with a seed drill is fast.

耪.origin: 耒 (plow) plus 旁: 耪 means to hoe or loosen the soil.

sentence.en: Grandpa goes to hoe the field.

耸.origin: 从 over 耳 (ear): rising up high, so 耸 means to tower or shrug up.

sentence.en: The tall building towers into the clouds.

耿.origin: 耳 (ear) plus 火 (fire): 耿 means bright, shining, or honest and devoted.

sentence.en: He is an honest and frank person.

聋.origin: 龙 over 耳 (ear): ears that cannot hear, so 聋 means deaf.

sentence.en: The old grandpa is a bit hard of hearing.

职.origin: 耳 (ear) plus 只: listening and doing one's duty, so 职 means job or duty.

sentence.en: Doctor is a good profession.

聘.origin: 耳 (ear) plus 甹: inviting someone to a post, so 聘 means to hire or engage.

sentence.en: The company is hiring workers.

聚.origin: 取 over people standing together: 聚 means to gather or assemble.

sentence.en: We are having a get-together this weekend.

肆.origin: 镸 plus 聿: 肆 means unrestrained or to indulge; it is also the formal form of four.

sentence.en: He speaks far too rudely.

肋.origin: ⺼ (flesh) plus 力: the curved bones in the chest, so 肋 means ribs.

sentence.en: People have many ribs.

肖.origin: Small marks over ⺼ (flesh): looking like someone in the flesh, so 肖 means to resemble.

sentence.en: A portrait hangs on the wall.

肘.origin: ⺼ (flesh) plus 寸: the joint in the middle of the arm, so 肘 means elbow.

sentence.en: He bumped his elbow.

肛.origin: ⺼ (flesh) plus 工: a body opening, so 肛 means anus.

sentence.en: The doctor examined the anus.
